metadata.description: Maps official 42 curriculum projects (legacy and new common core) to the preparation track/module structure defined in 42_lausanne_curriculum.json.
metadata.source_governance: Project names and ordering for the legacy common core are well-documented community knowledge. The new common core mapping is inferred from 42 Lausanne public infographics and community discussion — treat with appropriate caution.

piscine.label: La Piscine
piscine.confidence_rationale: The Piscine format is publicly documented by 42 Network.
piscine.description: Intensive 26-day selection process covering shell basics, C fundamentals, and peer learning discipline.
piscine.note: The Piscine is the entry gate. Our shell and C foundation modules are specifically designed to prepare for it.

legacy_common_core.description: The original 42 common core, historically C and Unix heavy. This mapping shows which preparation modules are most relevant for each project.

projects.label: Libft
projects.confidence_rationale: libft is universally the first graded project across all 42 campuses.
projects.description: Build a personal C library reimplementing standard libc functions.

projects.label: get_next_line
projects.confidence_rationale: Universally present in the early common core.
projects.description: Read a line from a file descriptor, handling buffer management.

projects.label: ft_printf
projects.confidence_rationale: Universally present in the early common core.
projects.description: Reimplement printf with variadic arguments.

projects.label: Born2beroot
projects.confidence_rationale: Well-documented across campuses as an early sysadmin project.
projects.description: Set up a virtual machine with strict security and service configuration.

projects.label: push_swap
projects.confidence_rationale: Present in both legacy and new common core.
projects.description: Sort a stack of integers using a limited set of operations with minimal moves.

projects.label: Pipex
projects.confidence_rationale: Standard Unix project in the legacy common core.
projects.description: Reproduce shell pipe behavior using fork, execve and dup2.

projects.label: Minishell
projects.confidence_rationale: Major milestone project, universally present.
projects.description: Build a simple shell with parsing, builtins, redirections and pipes.

projects.label: Philosophers
projects.confidence_rationale: Universally present as the concurrency introduction.
projects.description: Solve the dining philosophers problem using threads and mutexes.

projects.label: cub3D / miniRT
projects.confidence_rationale: One of the two is required; choice is campus-dependent.
projects.description: Build a raycaster (cub3D) or ray tracer (miniRT) rendering engine.

projects.label: CPP Modules (00-09)
projects.confidence_rationale: Standard CPP progression in the legacy common core.
projects.description: Series of 10 modules introducing C++ OOP, STL and design patterns.
projects.note: C++ builds on C knowledge but introduces OOP. No direct preparation module exists yet — future track candidate.

projects.label: NetPractice
projects.confidence_rationale: Present in both legacy and new common core.
projects.description: Configure network interfaces, routing tables and subnets in a simulator.
projects.note: Primarily conceptual. Shell tooling helps with network debugging commands (ping, ifconfig, netstat).

projects.label: Webserv / ft_irc
projects.confidence_rationale: One of the two is required in the legacy common core.
projects.description: Build an HTTP server (webserv) or IRC server (ft_irc) from scratch in C++.

projects.label: Inception
projects.confidence_rationale: Present in both legacy and new common core.
projects.description: Set up a multi-service infrastructure using Docker and docker-compose.

projects.label: ft_transcendence
projects.confidence_rationale: Final project in both legacy and new common core.
projects.description: Build a full-stack web application with real-time multiplayer features.
projects.note: Full-stack project. In the new curriculum, Python skills also apply. Preparation is broad rather than module-specific.

new_common_core_additions.description: Projects specific to or repositioned in the new common core. These names come from 42 Lausanne public materials and community interpretation — confidence is lower than legacy projects.

projects.label: Piscine Python
projects.confidence_rationale: Referenced in 42 Lausanne infographics. Exact scope is inferred from community mapping.
projects.description: Intensive Python bootcamp introducing the language within the common core.

projects.label: a maze ing
projects.confidence_rationale: Name appears in infographic. Scope and requirements are inferred.
projects.description: Likely an algorithmic/graphics project involving maze generation or solving.

projects.label: Codexion
projects.confidence_rationale: Name appears in infographic. No public description available.
projects.description: Unknown scope. Positioned mid-core, possibly algorithmic or systems-related.
projects.note: Insufficient information to map reliably. Listed for completeness.

projects.label: fly in
projects.confidence_rationale: Name appears in infographic. No public description available.
projects.description: Unknown scope. Possibly systems or network related.
projects.note: Insufficient information to map reliably. Listed for completeness.

projects.label: call me maybe
projects.confidence_rationale: Name appears in infographic. Likely network or IPC related based on naming.
projects.description: Possibly a networking or inter-process communication project.
projects.note: Mapping is speculative based on project name only.

projects.label: Pacman
projects.confidence_rationale: Name appears in infographic. Likely a graphics or game project.
projects.description: Possibly a game implementation project involving graphics and game logic.

projects.label: RAG against the machine
projects.confidence_rationale: Name clearly references RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation). Confirmed as AI-related in 42 Lausanne public materials.
projects.description: Build a retrieval-augmented generation system, applying AI and source governance principles.

projects.label: the answer protocol
projects.confidence_rationale: Name appears in infographic. Likely AI or protocol-related.
projects.description: Possibly an AI evaluation or protocol implementation project.
projects.note: Mapping is speculative. Positioned after RAG project in known ordering.

projects.label: agent smith
projects.confidence_rationale: Name references AI agents. Positioned late in the new common core, suggesting advanced AI work.
projects.description: Likely an agentic AI project involving autonomous agent design and orchestration.
